uplink.description: TER (0x04) datalog frame: 3 measurements (newest last) + trailing battery-percentage byte (32 bytes). Synthetic in-bounds outdoor reading; the first record uses a sub-zero temperature to exercise signed two's-complement decode. Newest record (08:30:00, +5.75 C, 72.5 %RH, 1013.25 hPa, atmospheric) is at the top level; the two older records are in history (newest-first). batteryPercent 87. Expected values produced by the ORACLE method: temperature/humidity/pressure cross-checked against reference/upstream-codec.js parseTER output.
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame. Expected temperature/humidity/pressure values confirmed against reference/upstream-codec.js (parseTER): upstream emits temperature_2/humidity_2/pressure_2 = 5.75/72.5/1013.25 for the newest record and the matching older values. Date bytes are encoded as the documented year-2000(7)/month(4)/day(5)/hour(5)/minute(6)/sec-div2(5) bitfield; upstream parseDate is locale-dependent (toLocaleString) so its formatted string is not used verbatim, only its decoded field values.

uplink.description: TER (0x04) frame with a single measurement + trailing battery byte (12 bytes). Synthetic in-bounds reading (+21.00 C, 45.0 %RH, 1009.00 hPa atmospheric, batteryPercent 95). Exercises the dynamic record reader: our codec recovers the single measurement and the battery byte, whereas rigid upstream parseTER misaligns and reports null for the absent 2nd/3rd records. No history (only one measurement).
uplink._note: Synthetic in-bounds frame. Expected temperature/humidity/pressure (21/45/1009) confirmed against reference/upstream-codec.js parseTER first-record output. Battery recovery is an authored improvement over the rigid upstream parser; field decode is unchanged.

uplink.description: Unsupported frame id (0x09 metering) is rejected. The MCF-LW12TER device only emits the TER (0x04) frame, so any other frame id is an error.
